metadata.title: SPARC Session Management and Cryptographic Protection

components.title: SPARC Application
components.description: SPARC (Systematic Policy and Regulatory Compliance) is a Rails application for managing NIST 800-53 compliance documentation. This component definition covers session management, transmission security, and cryptographic protection controls.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC enforces transmission confidentiality and integrity through FORCE_SSL in the production environment with HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) configured for a 1-year max-age, includeSubDomains, and preload directives. All HTTP requests are automatically redirected to HTTPS, ensuring TLS 1.2+ encryption for all data in transit. Outbound LDAP authentication (start_tls/simple_tls) verifies the directory server's certificate with OpenSSL VERIFY_PEER by default (#773), authenticating the server's identity rather than merely encrypting the channel; the trust anchor is the container/system CA store or an operator-supplied SPARC_LDAP_CA_FILE. Federation bundles (#372) carry an HMAC-SHA256 signature over a base64url-encoded canonical JSON payload, providing application-layer integrity that survives TLS termination at intermediate proxies. AuthoritativeSourceFetchService (#372) only fetches over https:// schemes; http:// hrefs are rejected outright.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in config/environments/production.rb (config.force_ssl = true with HSTS options); LDAP server-certificate verification in app/services/ldap_auth_service.rb (VERIFY_PEER unless SPARC_LDAP_TLS_VERIFY=false); federation signing in app/services/federation_bundle_signing_service.rb; HTTPS-only fetch in app/services/authoritative_source_fetch_service.rb. Tested by spec/services/ldap_auth_service_spec.rb, spec/services/federation_bundle_signing_service_spec.rb, spec/services/authoritative_source_fetch_service_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC manages cryptographic key establishment through Rails encrypted credentials, which store secrets encrypted with a master key. In production deployments, integration with AWS Key Management Service (KMS) provides hardware-backed key storage and lifecycle management for encryption keys. When SPARC_AWS_SECRETS_ENABLED is true, SECRET_KEY_BASE is managed via AWS Secrets Manager with KMS encryption and injected at boot. When SPARC_AWS_IAM_DB_AUTH is true, IAM database authentication uses IAM-signed tokens (SHA-256) for PostgreSQL connections instead of static passwords. The SparcKeyDerivation module (#372) introduces a per-instance master secret (SPARC_HASH) provisioned by sparc-iac into AWS Secrets Manager. SparcKeyDerivation derives purpose-specific symmetric keys via HKDF (ActiveSupport::KeyGenerator with SHA-256) from this master secret. Each derived key is bound to a versioned domain-separated purpose string ('sparc:v1:<purpose>') so two consumers never share key material. Subsystems using derived keys: federation peer service-token encryption (FederationPeer.token_encryptor), federation peer signing-secret encryption (FederationPeer.secret_encryptor).
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/lib/sparc_key_derivation.rb (master KDF), app/models/federation_peer.rb (subsystem keys), config/credentials/ (Rails encrypted credentials), config/initializers/00_aws_secrets.rb, and config/initializers/aws_db_auth.rb. SPARC_HASH provisioning tracked by risk-sentinel/sparc-iac issue #195. Tested by spec/lib/sparc_key_derivation_spec.rb, spec/models/federation_peer_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC employs FIPS-validated cryptographic algorithms: bcrypt for password hashing with configurable cost factor, SHA-256 for API token digest storage, RS256 (RSA with SHA-256) for JWT signature verification of Okta-issued tokens in API authentication, and TLS 1.2+ for transport encryption. The API validates JWTs using the RS256 algorithm with public keys fetched from the OIDC provider's JWKS endpoint. The Rails framework handles cryptographic operations through OpenSSL bindings that support FIPS-compliant algorithm implementations. Federation bundles (#372) are signed and verified using HMAC-SHA256 (FIPS 198-1) via OpenSSL::HMAC; canonical-JSON encoding ensures stable signatures regardless of object key ordering. Stored federation peer credentials use ActiveSupport::MessageEncryptor (AES-GCM authenticated encryption) keyed via SparcKeyDerivation.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/models/user.rb (bcrypt), app/models/api_token.rb (SHA-256), app/controllers/concerns/api_authentication.rb (JWT RS256 verification), config/environments/production.rb (TLS), app/services/federation_bundle_signing_service.rb (HMAC-SHA256), app/models/federation_peer.rb (AES-GCM via MessageEncryptor). Tested by spec/models/user_spec.rb, spec/models/api_token_spec.rb, spec/services/federation_bundle_signing_service_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC ensures session authenticity through session fixation prevention. The start_session method in the Authentication concern resets the Rails session before establishing a new authenticated session, generating a fresh session identifier. This prevents attackers from pre-setting session tokens to hijack authenticated sessions.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/controllers/concerns/authentication.rb:start_session (session reset before authentication). Tested by spec/requests/sessions_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC protects information at rest through PostgreSQL native encryption capabilities for database storage and AWS S3 Server-Side Encryption with KMS (SSE-KMS) for Active Storage file attachments. The production environment configures the Amazon S3 storage service for all uploaded documents, ensuring encryption at the storage layer. Application secrets (SECRET_KEY_BASE, database credentials) are KMS-encrypted in AWS Secrets Manager and injected at boot time — never stored on disk. Feature-gated by SPARC_AWS_SECRETS_ENABLED.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in config/environments/production.rb (config.active_storage.service = :amazon with SSE-KMS) and config/initializers/00_aws_secrets.rb. Database encryption managed by PostgreSQL infrastructure.
